data.title: DraftKings Stock Drops As Prediction Markets Gain A Regulatory Tailwind
data.description:
Shares of DraftKings Inc (NASDAQ:DKNG) are trading lower Friday after Guggenheim cut its price target to $42 from $45 and after the Commodity Futures Trading Commission said it will draft "clear rules" for prediction markets, scrapping proposals that targeted sports- and politics-linked event contracts.

Here’s what investors need to know.

  • DraftKings stock is showing notable weakness. Why is DKNG stock falling?

How Regulatory Clarity Could Reshape DraftKings’ Future

Guggenheim has lowered its price target on DraftKings to $42 from $45 while maintaining a Buy rating on the shares. This adjustment follows the CFTC’s announcement that it will draft “clear rules” for prediction markets, which includes withdrawing a proposal that prohibited sports and politics-related contracts.

The CFTC’s Chair, Michael S. Selig, emphasized the need for regulatory clarity, stating that the existing framework has been difficult to apply and has failed market participants. This policy reversal could impact how DraftKings and other companies in the sector operate, potentially leading to increased competition or changes in market dynamics.

Is Robinhood The Most Dangerous Competitor For DKNG?

Robinhood has processed more than 11 billion contracts and is generating roughly $100 million in annualized revenue from prediction markets.

Exchange-style Pricing Pressures Sportsbook

Event contracts are priced in cents, settle at $0 or $1, and can be sold before the game ends, mechanics that look more like trading than betting. Robinhood has charged as little as 1 cent per contract.

Kalshi payouts also often beat DraftKings, signaling potential margin compression. Kalshi has also recently expanded into spreads, totals and player props which are high-margin categories for sportsbooks.

What’s Next?

Kalshi's record NFL playoff volume, including via Robinhood, reinforces the market-share risk, while state actions add uncertainty, including a Massachusetts ruling forced Kalshi to block sports contracts in the state. DraftKings reports earnings Feb. 12.

DraftKings Technical Analysis

Currently, DraftKings is trading 17.3% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 20.9% below its 100-day SMA, indicating a bearish trend. Over the past 12 months, shares have decreased by 35.69%, and they are currently positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs, reflecting ongoing weakness in the stock.

The RSI is at 33.64, which is considered neutral territory, while the MACD is below its signal line, indicating bearish pressure on the stock. The combination of neutral RSI and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum, reflecting uncertainty in the stock’s near-term outlook.

  • Key Resistance: $32.00
  • Key Support: $26.00

DKNG’s Business Model

DraftKings got its start in 2012 as an innovator in daily fantasy sports. Following a Supreme Court ruling in 2018 that allowed states to legalize online sports wagering, the company expanded into online sports and casino gambling, where it generally holds the number-two or -three revenue share position across states where it competes.

Currently, DraftKings operates in 28 states for online or retail sports betting and in five states for i-gaming, reaching around 40% of Canada’s population. The company’s recent challenges, including regulatory shifts and market pressures, highlight the competitive landscape in which it operates.

DraftKings Earnings Preview

Investors are looking ahead to the next earnings report on Feb. 12.

  • EPS Estimate: 37 cents (Up from 14 cents YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $1.96 billion (Up from $1.39 billion YoY)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $46.88. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Stifel: Buy (Lowers Target to $44.00) (Jan. 30)
  • Guggenheim: Buy (Lowers Target to $42.00) (Jan. 29)
  • Morgan Stanley: Overweight (Raises Target to $53.00) (Jan. 16)

Top ETF Exposure

Significance: Because DKNG carries significant weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows for these ETFs will likely force automatic buying or selling of the stock.

DKNG Shares Slide Friday

DKNG Price Action: DraftKings shares were down 8.55% at $27.38 at the time of publication on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Bitcoin Below $84,000 As Ethereum, XRP, Dogecoin Extend Losses On Heavy ETF Outflows
data.description:
Bitcoin is struggling to hold $84,000 as heavy U.S. spot ETF outflows signal institutional caution and fuel another wave of liquidations.

Notable Statistics:

  • Coinglass data shows 203,945 traders were liquidated in the past 24 hours for $1.43 billion.        
  • In the past 24 hours, top losers include River, Tether Gold and PAX Gold.

Notable Developments:

Trader Notes: Crypto trader Jelle said most near-term downside liquidity has already been cleared, but a major liquidity pool remains near the $80,000 level. Bitcoin's next move will depend on the daily and weekly closes.

KillaXBT pointed to seasonal patterns, noting that as Feb. 14 approaches, Bitcoin has historically seen drops of more than 5% within a two-week window.

From an algorithmic perspective, he said short-term consolidation or upside retests are likely before a further leg lower.

Despite that potential bounce, KillaXBT said the broader trend remains bearish and he is not taking long positions.

Whale Factor noted Bitcoin has now printed five consecutive red monthly candles, a level of weakness last seen near the 2018 cycle bottom.

He added that trader exhaustion, deeply negative sentiment and the return of "crypto is dead" narratives are conditions that have historically preceded major inflection points.

While the trend remains fragile, Whale Factor said this is a period where market participants should stay alert.

Image: Shutterstock

Order flow analytics analyze real-time buying and selling trends by examining the volume, timing, and order size across both retail and institutional traders. data.title: Is NNN's 5.6% Dividend Yield Safe? A Beginner's Checklist
data.description:
If you’re new to dividend investing, a 5.6% yield can look attractive—especially when it comes from a company that has raised its payout for 36 consecutive years. But yield alone doesn’t tell the full story. The question isn’t just “how much does it pay?” but “can it keep paying?”

But even stable-looking companies can cut dividends when the math stops working. This checklist walks through four metrics that help determine whether NNN’s dividend sits on solid ground—or shaky soil.

What Is a Net Lease REIT?

Before diving into the numbers, it helps to understand NNN’s business model.

In a typical lease, landlords pay for property taxes, insurance, and maintenance. In a net lease, tenants pay those costs instead. This structure gives NNN predictable cash flows with minimal operating expenses—ideal for sustaining dividends.

NNN’s tenants sign leases averaging 10.1 years in length, with built-in annual rent increases averaging 1.5%. This creates a long runway of predictable income, assuming tenants stay healthy.

Now, let’s check the numbers.

1. Credit Rating: BBB+ (S&P) / Baa1 (Moody’s)

A credit rating measures a company’s ability to repay debt. Ratings of BBB- and above are considered “investment grade”—meaning institutional investors like pension funds and insurance companies can hold these bonds.

NNN holds a BBB+ rating from S&P and Baa1 from Moody’s (latest publicly confirmed ratings). Both are solidly investment grade.

Why does this matter for dividends? If a REIT’s rating falls below BBB-, two things happen:

  1. Borrowing costs rise sharply
  2. Many institutional investors are forced to sell

This “forced selling” can trigger a downward spiral in stock price and make it harder for the company to raise capital. Dividend cuts often follow.

NNN sits two notches above that BBB- cliff. It’s not a wide buffer, but it’s a buffer.

Source: S&P Global Ratings (June 2025 affirmation)

2. AFFO Payout Ratio: 70%

AFFO stands for Adjusted Funds From Operations. Think of it as the cash a REIT generates after maintaining its properties—essentially, the money available to pay dividends.

In Q3 2025, NNN reported AFFO of $0.86 per share and paid a $0.60 quarterly dividend. Divide 0.60 by 0.86, and you get a 70% payout ratio.

What does 70% mean? It means NNN keeps 30 cents of every dollar it earns. That retained cash can be used to pay down debt, acquire new properties, or cushion against unexpected vacancies.

Payout ratios above 85% start raising red flags. At 90% or higher, there’s little room for error—one bad quarter could force a dividend cut.

At 70%, NNN has a 15-percentage-point cushion before reaching dangerous territory.

Source: NNN REIT Q3 2025 Earnings Release (November 4, 2025)

3. Dividend Track Record: 36 Years of Consecutive Increases

NNN has increased its annual dividend every year since 1989. NNN is one of only three publicly traded REITs to have increased annual dividends for 36 or more consecutive years.

A long track record doesn’t guarantee future increases. Companies can—and do—break their streaks when conditions deteriorate. But 36 years of consecutive raises tells you something about management’s priorities: they view the dividend as sacred.

This matters because dividend cuts destroy shareholder trust. Once a company cuts, investors often assume another cut is coming. Stock prices fall, cost of capital rises, and a negative cycle begins.

NNN’s management has navigated recessions, interest rate spikes, and tenant bankruptcies without breaking the streak. That history earns some benefit of the doubt.

Source: NNN REIT Official Announcement

4. Occupancy: 97.5%

Occupancy measures the percentage of NNN’s properties that are currently leased and generating rent. As of September 30, 2025, that number stood at 97.5%.

This is slightly below NNN’s historical average, which typically hovers around 98-99%. The dip came from proactive asset repositioning—essentially, NNN choosing to exit certain underperforming properties rather than waiting for problems to escalate.

Management expects occupancy to recover above 98% by year-end 2025. This still leaves NNN well within its historical comfort range.

Why does occupancy matter? Empty properties generate zero rent but still incur costs. If occupancy drops below 95%, the math starts working against dividend sustainability.

At 97.5%, NNN maintains a 2.5-percentage-point buffer above that threshold.

Source: NNN REIT Q3 2025 Earnings Release

Bonus Check: Debt Maturity Profile

One hidden risk for REITs is debt refinancing. If a company has billions coming due next year, it might have to refinance at much higher rates—squeezing cash flow and threatening dividends.

NNN is well-positioned here. The weighted average maturity of its debt is approximately 10.7 years as of Q3 2025. This means NNN has locked in its borrowing costs for over a decade. When the Fed raises rates, NNN doesn’t feel the pain immediately.

Source: NNN REIT Q3 2025 Earnings Release

What Would Change This Picture?

The checklist looks solid today. But conditions change. Here’s what to watch:

None of these conditions exist today. But they’re the early warning signals that separate proactive investors from surprised ones.

The Checklist Summary

Bottom Line

NNN’s 5.6% yield comes with real structural support: an investment-grade credit rating, a conservative 70% payout ratio, a 36-year dividend growth streak, and occupancy that remains above 97%.

The buffers aren’t enormous. Two notches of credit cushion isn’t huge. But for a company with NNN’s track record and business model, the current setup suggests the dividend is sustainable—unless something fundamental changes.

For beginners, this checklist provides a repeatable way to evaluate dividend safety—before relying on yield alone.

Benzinga Disclaimer: This article is from an unpaid external contributor. It does not represent Benzinga’s reporting and has not been edited for content or accuracy.

data.title: Lumentum Holdings Stock: A Deep Dive Into Analyst Perspectives (16 Ratings)
data.description:
Analysts' ratings for Lumentum Holdings (NASDAQ:LITE) over the last quarter vary from bullish to bearish, as provided by 16 analysts.

The following table encapsulates their recent ratings, offering a glimpse into the evolving sentiments over the past 30 days and comparing them to the preceding months.

Analysts have set 12-month price targets for Lumentum Holdings, revealing an average target of $322.94, a high estimate of $470.00, and a low estimate of $147.00. This upward trend is evident, with the current average reflecting a 42.77% increase from the previous average price target of $226.20.

Deciphering Analyst Ratings: An In-Depth Analysis

A clear picture of Lumentum Holdings's perception among financial experts is painted with a thorough analysis of recent analyst actions. The summary below outlines key analysts, their recent evaluations, and adjustments to ratings and price targets.

Key Insights:

Analyzing these analyst evaluations alongside relevant financial metrics can provide a comprehensive view of Lumentum Holdings's market position. Stay informed and make data-driven decisions with the assistance of our Ratings Table.

Stay up to date on Lumentum Holdings analyst ratings.

All You Need to Know About Lumentum Holdings

Financial Milestones: Lumentum Holdings's Journey

Market Capitalization Analysis: With an elevated market capitalization, the company stands out above industry averages, showcasing substantial size and market acknowledgment.

Positive Revenue Trend: Examining Lumentum Holdings's financials over 3M reveals a positive narrative. The company achieved a noteworthy revenue growth rate of 58.44% as of 30 September, 2025, showcasing a substantial increase in top-line earnings. As compared to competitors, the company surpassed expectations with a growth rate higher than the average among peers in the Information Technology sector.

Net Margin: Lumentum Holdings's net margin excels beyond industry benchmarks, reaching 0.79%. This signifies efficient cost management and strong financial health.

Return on Equity (ROE): Lumentum Holdings's ROE is below industry standards, pointing towards difficulties in efficiently utilizing equity capital. With an ROE of 0.44%, the company may encounter challenges in delivering satisfactory returns for shareholders.

Return on Assets (ROA): Lumentum Holdings's ROA is below industry averages, indicating potential challenges in efficiently utilizing assets. With an ROA of 0.1%, the company may face hurdles in achieving optimal financial returns.

Debt Management: Lumentum Holdings's debt-to-equity ratio surpasses industry norms, standing at 4.2. This suggests the company carries a substantial amount of debt, posing potential financial challenges.

Analyst Ratings: Simplified

Experts in banking and financial systems, analysts specialize in reporting for specific stocks or defined sectors. Their comprehensive research involves attending company conference calls and meetings, analyzing financial statements, and engaging with insiders to generate what are known as analyst ratings for stocks. Typically, analysts assess and rate each stock once per quarter.

Some analysts also offer predictions for helpful metrics such as earnings, revenue, and growth estimates to provide further guidance as to what to do with certain tickers. It is important to keep in mind that while stock and sector analysts are specialists, they are also human and can only forecast their beliefs to traders.

This article was generated by Benzinga's automated content engine and reviewed by an editor.
